title: permessage-deflate context takeover reset drops bytes on very large messages
body: With server-side EnableWriteCompression enabled and no context takeover, decompressNoContextTakeover() appends the RFC tail bytes via io.MultiReader before resetting the shared flate.Reader. On very large multi-fragment messages we're occasionally seeing truncated payloads client-side. Is the pooled reader reset guaranteed to fully drain before being reused?

title: Pure-Go mask fallback used even when an ARM64 optimized path would help
body: mask_safe.go's pure-Go XOR loop is used as the portable fallback, but on arm64 servers we're masking a lot of outbound frames and it shows up in profiles. Is there any interest in an arm64 SIMD-ish fast path similar to what exists for amd64, or is mask.go intentionally kept architecture-agnostic?

title: Dialer proxy support ignores NO_PROXY for websocket URLs
body: proxy.go picks up HTTP_PROXY/HTTPS_PROXY from the environment when dialing ws:// and wss:// URLs, but NO_PROXY exclusions don't seem to be honored, so internal websocket endpoints get routed through the proxy anyway. Expected internal hosts listed in NO_PROXY to bypass the proxy like they do for plain HTTP clients.

title: Chat example hub never times out a slow client's outbound write
body: In examples/chat/hub.go, Hub.run() broadcasts to every registered client's send channel, but if a client's writePump is stuck, the broadcast loop just drops messages for that client silently (buffer full -> close). It'd help the example to demonstrate a write deadline via SetWriteDeadline so newcomers copying this pattern don't miss it.

title: ReadMessage allocates a fresh buffer per call instead of reusing readBuf
body: Under high message-rate load, conn.go's NextReader/ReadMessage path appears to allocate rather than reuse the connection's read buffer, showing up as GC pressure in pprof for a chat relay pushing thousands of small messages/sec. Is there a supported way to reuse buffers across ReadMessage calls?
